title: white
comment: A colorpoint cat holds only its head around a corner — neither committed to entering the room nor quite willing to leave. The negotiation between curiosity and caution, visible in cross-section.

title: cat
comment: An orange tabby fully elongated on a patterned bedspread, paws extended, head lowered. This is the stretch that means staying — not the stretch of arriving, but the one that settles a thing.

title: Tent
comment: A gray tabby at the entrance of a pop-up tent, eyes wide and fixed on something outside the frame. It has all the indoor furniture it needs. It chose the tent.

title: SWS
comment: A black and white cat under a wooden table, looking directly at the camera with wide, alert eyes. Under furniture is not hiding — it's the cat's preferred architecture for observing a room.

title: cat
comment: A light gray cat lying on its back, body fully extended, head tilted upward at the camera. Belly exposure is the most deliberate kind of trust. It offers this freely.

title: walking
comment: A stocky gray and white cat moving directly toward the camera, body low and forward, expression fixed. It has somewhere to be. The camera is not a priority.

title: Fierce
comment: A tabby on a blue rug, looking upward through alert green eyes. Tagged Fierce on the photo site. The calm of something that has already decided and is simply waiting for you to catch up.

title: sws
comment: A tabby on a striped blanket, eyes half-closed, body settled. The moment just before sleep when a cat still appears to be watching but has already stopped caring.

title: catstickerss
comment: A dense-furred tabby held against someone's light pink shirt, head squared directly at the camera. Being held hasn't changed its attention — it's still taking everything in.

title: Black and white
comment: A black and white cat looking directly at the camera, ears forward, eyes wide and yellow-green. The posture of an animal that has just identified something and is deciding whether it's interesting.

title: tortie
comment: A calico near a screen door, tail curved beside it, gaze directed outward. Whatever is on the other side of that door has been tracked for some time.

title: sleepy
comment: A gray tabby on a plaid blanket, body relaxed but eyes sharp and forward. The look that means the body has stopped but the attention hasn't.

title: close
comment: A tuxedo cat photographed from below at close range, head tilted back, eyes partially closed. It looks like someone who has decided you're fine.

title: sleep
comment: An orange tabby on its back across someone's lap, front paws up, wholly at ease. Lap cats that go belly-up are making a specific declaration. This one is making it clearly.

title: cat
comment: A dark tabby on a desk, leaning forward over papers and supplies, head lowered. Whatever work was happening on this desk has been superseded.

title: black
comment: A black cat sitting upright inside an open cardboard box, amber eyes bright, ears forward. The box is open. It chose to be here.

title: cat
comment: A brown and white cat curled in a plastic carrier, looking directly at the camera. It knows what the carrier means. It's facing it anyway.

title: persian
comment: A fluffy white cat wearing turquoise sunglasses in front of a psychedelic geometric backdrop. The expression is entirely neutral. The glasses are doing their best.

title: cute
comment: A gray tabby among books and papers on a desk, looking at the camera with a mildly skeptical expression. The workspace has been claimed. The cat will receive your questions.

title: orange
comment: An orange tabby peering through a frame of pink flowers, eyes wide, backlit in warm gold. Something put it there — a garden, a border, an accident — and it accepted the arrangement.

title: cat
comment: A light-colored cat in dim light, pupils fully dilated, staring directly at the camera. The purple cast and the grain of the photo make this look like surveillance. It looks back the same way.

title: maine coon
comment: A tuxedo cat on a lap, head tilted toward the camera, tongue slightly out. The tongue is the detail — caught mid-lick or mid-exhale, the one moment it stopped managing its appearance.

title: cat
comment: An orange and white tabby stretched full-length on a cushion, front paws extended, eyes in a contented half-close. The posture of something that has found exactly where it wants to be and is staying.

title: evil
comment: A broad-faced orange tabby rests its head on a computer mouse, eyes half-lidded. Tagged evil on the photo site. It does not look evil. It looks settled.

title: italian st
comment: A tabby spread across a blue Italian textbook, head tilted back, eyes nearly closed. Indifferent to the study materials beneath it in the way cats are indifferent to everything they have claimed.

title: funny
comment: An orange and white cat sitting upright in an oversized brown cowboy hat, looking directly at the camera with the mild attentiveness of an animal that has accepted its situation without opinion.

title: cat
comment: A cream-colored cat outdoors with eyes closed and head tilted slightly upward — the deliberate posture of active basking, not just exposure to sun.

title: table
comment: The tabby on the bench has its mouth open mid-meow, caught in the act of announcing something to the world while its companions remain silent and watchful. It's the only one demanding attention, the only one willing to break the peace of this shaded gathering.

title: tabby
comment: This cat is completely relaxed on the bench, sprawled with its front legs extended, utterly indifferent to the camera despite making direct eye contact. There's something unselfconscious about the pose—it's the comfort of a cat that owns its space.

title: cat
comment: This cat has the arrested, slightly affronted expression of someone who's been interrupted mid-play and is deciding whether the interruption was worth it. The carefully arranged toys suggest a human trying to engineer fun, while the cat's fixed stare implies it will be the judge of that.

title: Shhhsh
comment: The cat has pressed its face into dark fabric with complete surrender, its pink nose flattened and eyes shut tight in a way that suggests either exhaustion or a deliberate attempt to block out the world. There's something unguarded about the posture—a heaviness that reads less like contentment and more like resignation or fatigue.

title: cat
comment: This cat is intently focused on something just out of frame, its whole body angled toward the tree trunk with the concentrated stillness of a hunter. There's a tautness in its posture that suggests it's caught between the urge to pounce and the caution of being tethered.

title: cat
comment: This cat has the exhausted, trapped expression of someone forced to participate in a board game they didn't agree to play. The slight upturn of its mouth reads less like contentment and more like resignation to an evening of Scrabble.

title: cat
comment: This cat has completely surrendered to the floor, sprawled in that boneless way that suggests either profound contentment or the heat has defeated all resistance. There's something almost pathetic about how it's stretched itself thin against the wood, as if it's trying to become part of the furniture.

title: cute
comment: This cat is yawning with an intensity that suggests either genuine exhaustion or complete indifference to being filmed. There's something unselfconscious about the moment—the animal mid-stretch, unperformed, just existing in its own time.

title: Box
comment: This cat is locked in an intense stare of pure want, eyes wide and unblinking, clearly decided that whatever is on that counter belongs to it now. The smaller black cat below seems to be offering moral support for the heist.

title: hash_brown
comment: This cat's expression is one of pure displeasure at being stuffed into a McDonald's breakfast wrapper, eyes locked forward with the resigned irritation of something that has accepted its temporary imprisonment but will absolutely remember it. The disconnect between the cheerful "Hash Brown" branding and the cat's flat, unimpressed stare suggests whoever took this photo is about to face swift and meaningful consequences.

title: cat
comment: This cat has surrendered completely to the couch, sprawled on its back in that particular state of domestic confidence where it knows exactly how much space it can claim. There's something about the way one paw dangles and the tail curls—it's the posture of an animal that has decided the furniture exists for its comfort alone.

title: cat
comment: This cat has completely surrendered to the couch, sprawled in that particular boneless state where a cat stops pretending to have a skeleton. The way its head lolls back and one paw dangles suggests it's either deeply asleep or has reached that point of summer heat where staying awake feels like too much effort.
